name: scout-boundaries
title: Scope Contract Map
description: Define the exact goal, scope boundary, constraints, and done condition before planning or specification work. Use when the request is still too broad to turn directly into a plan or spec.
purpose: Reduce a broad request to an explicit scope contract that downstream planning or spec skills can consume.
when_to_use: Need to lock goal, scope, constraints, and done condition before planning.
Need to separate in-scope, out-of-scope, and acceptance boundary.
Need a bounded contract before writing specs or tasks.
do_not_use: Already have a precise scope contract.
Need direct implementation or review work.
Need full planning artifacts rather than scope normalization only.

required_inputs.name: REQUEST
required_inputs.description: Original user request or work item.

required_inputs.name: KNOWN_CONSTRAINTS
required_inputs.description: Explicit constraints or non-goals already known.

structured_outputs.name: IN_SCOPE
structured_outputs.description: Items or paths inside the scope boundary.

neutrality_rules: Separate explicit constraints from inferred assumptions.
Mark unresolved scope edges instead of silently deciding them.
Do not turn preference into scope without evidence.
default_program: [stages: preflight>detect>analyze>plan>handoff>audit | scope: repo|diff|paths(glob,...) | policy: evidence,quality-gates{docs},deterministic-output | lens: minto-pyramid | output: md(contract=v1)]
procedure: Normalize the request into one clear goal statement.
Draw the in-scope and out-of-scope boundary explicitly.
Convert constraints into a concrete done condition.
Return a stable scope contract for planner or spec.
mandatory_rules: Keep the output contract-first and compact.
Do not emit a full implementation plan here.
example_invocation:
$scout-boundaries
REQUEST: simplify the auth module without behavior change
TARGET_SCOPE: src/auth
lens_rationale: Scope contracts should state the answer first, then bound in-scope, out-of-scope, and done criteria.
